decisions.title_en: OpenAI launches $4B Deployment Company with TPG/Bain/Brookfield + acquires Tomoro for 150 FDEs
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Major new venture: OpenAI launches majority-owned $4B+ enterprise AI services company with 19 partners (TPG-led, McKinsey/Bain/Capgemini etc.) + simultaneously acquires Scottish AI consulting firm Tomoro for ~150 Forward Deployed Engineers. Direct competitive response to Anthropic's $1.5B Wall Street JV from May 5.

decisions.title_en: Cerebras upsizes IPO to $4.8B at $48.8B valuation as price range jumps to $150-160
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Cerebras filed updated S-1 May 11 raising IPO price range from $115-125 to $150-160 (+30%) and offering 30M shares (was 28M); new fully-diluted valuation up to $48.8B (vs $23B Feb round). On track to be largest 2026 IPO globally; OpenAI is largest customer with $20B+ contract + warrants on 33M shares.

decisions.title_en: Trump invites Musk, Cook, Fink, Boeing's Ortberg to May 13-15 Beijing summit; Nvidia's Huang notably absent
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. White House confirmed May 11 the CEO delegation accompanying Trump on May 13-15 Beijing state visit: Musk (Tesla/SpaceX/xAI), Cook (Apple), Fink (BlackRock), Ortberg (Boeing). Notably absent: Jensen Huang despite Nvidia being central to AI chip export controls (key summit topic). Major signaling event for US-China tech diplomacy.

decisions.title_en: Alphabet plans first-ever yen Samurai bond + raises 2026 capex outlook to $190B
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. Bloomberg May 11 exclusive: Alphabet mandates Mizuho/BofA/MS for debut yen-denominated senior unsecured bond; capex outlook revised up to $180-190B (was $185B; was double 2025's). Follows ~$17B EUR/CAD raise earlier in May. Concrete sign that even net-cash hyperscalers are now currency-diversifying debt to fund AI buildout.

decisions.title_en: Satya Nadella testifies in Musk v. Altman trial: 'didn't want Microsoft to be next IBM'
decisions.reason: Previously published 5-4 (Brockman Day 5/$30B stake), 5-5 (Brockman Day 6/Mars-city), 5-7 (Zilis testimony, Tesla board offer). New on 5-11: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella took the stand, said Musk never raised concerns about MS/OpenAI investment, internal email worried about Microsoft becoming 'the next IBM'; under cross said never demanded Altman reinstatement, was unaware of OpenAI nonprofit having any full-time staff before March 2026. Altman to testify Tuesday (5-12), advisory verdict expected week of 5-18.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI grants EU institutions access to GPT-5.5-Cyber; Anthropic still withholding Mythos from EU
decisions.reason: Previously published 5-7 (initial launch to vetted US 'authorized defenders'). New on 5-11: OpenAI announced EU access — businesses, governments, EU institutions added; OpenAI for Countries head George Osborne quoted; Anthropic explicitly contrasted as still holding back Mythos from EU despite earlier requests (returning the 5-4 'Eurogroup demands Mythos access' story to the news cycle). Geographic expansion + competitive positioning vs Anthropic = substantively new.

decisions.title_en: monday.com Q1 2026: revenue $351M (+24% YoY) beats; AI Work Platform GA + 10% of net new ARR from AI
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. monday.com (MNDY) reported Q1 2026 May 11: revenue $351M (+24% YoY, +$12M beat), record $49M operating profit, EPS $1.15 (+$0.22 beat), stock +20.9% intraday. Simultaneously launched the AI Work Platform with native agents + new consumption-based AI pricing — first major SaaS pure-play to fully repackage around agentic pricing. AI products = 10% of net new ARR.
